response_text: The onboarding flow is confusing. I had no idea what to do after signing up.
Search works well but the filters reset every time I navigate away, which is really frustrating.
I love the dashboard. Everything I need is right there without having to dig around.
The export function is broken. I've reported it twice and nothing has happened.
The API documentation is clear and the examples actually work, which is rare.
Error messages are completely unhelpful. 'Something went wrong' tells me nothing about what to do next.
Keyboard navigation is broken on the settings page. I use a keyboard only and I'm stuck.
The new tagging system has completely changed how my team synthesizes research. Huge improvement.
Mobile app is unusable. Text is tiny, buttons are too close together, and I accidentally delete things constantly.
Response time on the dashboard is slow when you have more than 50 items. Everything else is fast.
The undo history only goes back ten steps. On a complex diagram that's not nearly enough.
Tooltips are helpful but they disappear too fast. I have to hover multiple times to read them.
I've recommended this tool to three other teams. The participant management features alone are worth it.
Cannot figure out how to transfer ownership of a project. Spent 40 minutes looking. Gave up.
The color picker finally remembers my brand colors. Small thing but it saves me time every day.
Video clips are great but the transcript sync is off by a few seconds, which makes clipping awkward.
Collaboration features lag when more than three people are in the same file. We've had conflicts.
The template library saved us weeks of setup. I wish we'd found this tool two years ago.
Loading states need work. The spinner gives no indication of progress or estimated time.
Notifications are out of control. I get emails, in-app pings, and Slack messages for every comment.
The component variants feature is excellent. It's changed how our whole team builds design systems.
Search doesn't find things in notes, only in tags. That's a fundamental limitation for how we work.
The WYSIWYG editor is inconsistent. Sometimes pasted text keeps formatting, sometimes it strips it.
Prototyping has gotten fast enough that I can test interactions the same day I design them.
Sharing findings with stakeholders is finally easy. The read-only view is clean and professional.
Billing section is impossible to find. Had to ask support where it was. It's buried four levels deep.
Auto-layout is powerful but the learning curve is steep. Took me a week to feel confident.
The insight clustering tool is interesting but I don't fully trust it yet. I still verify manually.
Documentation is thorough and actually kept up to date, which is unusual. Big trust builder.
Version history is almost unusable. No descriptions, no way to tell which version has what. Just dates.
The participant consent tracking feature has simplified our IRB process considerably.
Onboarding tooltip sequence skips steps if you click too fast. I missed setting up integrations entirely.
Real-time collaboration is smooth. We've replaced most of our design review meetings with async comments.
Bulk tagging was removed in the last update with no explanation. That was a core part of our workflow.
The publishing workflow is exactly what our team needed. Draft, review, publish — all in one place.
